title: Corticotropin, Repository Injection: MedlinePlus Drug Information
text:
on or give it to your child if you or your child have any of these conditions. tell your doctor if you know that you have any type of infection, if you have a fever, cough, vomiting, diarrhea, flu-symptoms, or any other signs of infection, or if you have a family member who has an infection or signs of infection. Also tell your doctor if you have tuberculosis (TB; a severe lung infection), if you know that you have been exposed to TB, or if you have ever had a positive skin test for TB. Also tell your doctor if you have or have ever had diabetes, an underactive thyroid gland, conditions that affect your nerves or muscles such as myasthenia gravis (MG; a condition that causes weakness of certain muscles), problems with your stomach or intestines, emotional problems, psychosis (difficulty recognizing reality), or liver or kidney disease. tell your doctor if you are pregnant, plan to become pregnant, or are breast-feeding. If you become pregnant while using corticotropin repository injection, call your doctor.
if you are having surgery, including dental surgery, or need emergency medical treatment, tell the doctor, dentist, or medical staff that you are using corticotropin repository injection.
